Title: The homeotic gene PhDEF regulates production of volatiles in petunia flowers by activating EOBI and EOBII

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step to phenylpropanoid volatile pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies core precursor flux toward floral benzenoid/phenylpropanoid volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported as transcriptionally activated by PhDEF
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports production of phenylpropanoid floral volatiles; its transcript is reduced when PhDEF is suppressed and its promoter is activated by PhDEF
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in petals of mature flowers; evaluated at 2DPA and 8 PM
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flower developmental stage around anthesis/2DPA; evening-associated scent production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in PhDEF-VIGS petals; promoter activation assay with PALp:DsRed plus 35S:PhDEF; volatile profiling by GC-MS after PhDEF suppression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL transcript was significantly reduced in PhDEF-suppressed petals. 2. PhDEF activated the PAL promoter in petals and leaves. 3. PAL is part of the PhDEF-controlled flux into floral phenylpropanoid volatiles.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not establish PAL as the sole direct target; activation may be direct or indirect although promoter activation and predicted CArG-like motifs support direct regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PAL promoter, native promoter in reporter assay

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms a key volatile intermediate for C6-C2 floral scent compounds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-phenylalanine to phenylacetaldehyde in the floral scent pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in C6-C2 volatile formation, transcriptionally activated by PhDEF
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of C6-C2 floral volatiles; transcript level decreased upon PhDEF suppression and promoter was activated by PhDEF
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in petals of mature flowers; evaluated at 2DPA and 8 PM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in PhDEF-VIGS petals; promoter activation assay with PAASp:DsRed plus 35S:PhDEF; GC-MS volatile profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAAS transcript decreased in PhDEF-suppressed petals. 2. PhDEF activated the PAAS promoter in petals and leaves. 3. PAAS is a PhDEF-controlled enzyme supporting C6-C2 floral volatile production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct DNA binding was not proven; regulation is supported by promoter activation and presence of predicted CArG-like motifs.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PAAS promoter, native promoter in reporter assay

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression increases during bud growth, peaks at 1DPA, remains high until 3DPA, declines by 4DPA; about 2-fold higher at 8 PM than 9 AM
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in mature petals around anthesis to 3DPA and in evening
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental maturation of petals; diurnal/evening phase
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: EOBI; EOBII; PAL; PAAS; PhGLO1; PhGLO2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of phenylpropanoid volatile biosynthesis and emission machinery
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates EOBI, EOBII, PAL, and PAAS promoters; positively maintains transcript levels of multiple scent regulators and pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive autoregulation suggested; activates its own promoter and promotes PhGLO1/2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: DEF–GLO heterodimer known for autoregulation/petal identity, but volatile regulation appears GLO-independent in mature flowers
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive master regulator of floral phenylpropanoid volatile production and emission
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master positive regulator linking petal identity program to floral scent production in mature flowers
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Anthesis to 2DPA/3DPA; evening; VIGS suppression analyses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Local transient VIGS knockdown; GC-MS of emitted/internal volatiles; RT-qPCR of regulators and biosynthetic genes; promoter activation assays in petals and leaves
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhDEF expression peaks in mature petals and rises in the evening in parallel with scent production. 2. VIGS suppression of PhDEF reduced total emitted volatiles and internal pools across C6-C1, C6-C2, and C6-C3 phenylpropanoid branches, while terpenoid pinene was unaffected. 3. PhDEF suppression downregulated EOBII, EOBI, ODO1, PH4, ABCG1/11/12 and multiple shikimate/phenylpropanoid genes. 4. PhDEF activated EOBI, EOBII, PAL, and PAAS promoters, supporting a direct/indirect transcriptional control mechanism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate master regulator for engineering floral scent intensity and coordinating ornamental pollinator-attraction traits; could be stacked with scent-pathway genes, but developmental roles require caution.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because PhDEF is a homeotic gene controlling petal identity and other floral traits, constitutive manipulation may risk pleiotropic developmental effects; transient mature-tissue targeting may mitigate this.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for ectopic expression in promoter activation assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PhDEF suppression had no obvious petal morphological changes in the transient assay window; effect on volatiles appears independent of PhGLO1/2 in mature flowers; direct binding to downstream promoters remains to be proven despite predicted CArG motifs.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Scent-related regulator expressed in mature petals; expression parallels evening scent production
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in petals of mature flowers; reduced after PhDEF suppression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Scent-related biosynthetic genes in phenylpropanoid pathway
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of floral scent biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive scent regulator; transcript reduced upon PhDEF suppression and promoter activated by PhDEF
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes floral scent production; positioned downstream of PhDEF
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary positive scent regulator activated by PhDEF
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR after PhDEF suppression; promoter activation assay using EOBIp:DsRed with 35S:PhDEF
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EOBI transcript was significantly reduced in PhDEF-suppressed petals. 2. PhDEF activated the EOBI promoter in petals and leaves. 3. EOBI is a downstream regulatory node through which PhDEF promotes phenylpropanoid scent biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: EOBI promoter in reporter assay; 35S:PhDEF effector
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as core because the paper directly demonstrates promoter activation by PhDEF and its transcript response in PhDEF-suppressed flowers.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Scent-related regulator expressed in mature petals; expression parallels evening scent production
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in petals of mature flowers; reduced after PhDEF suppression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: EOBI and scent-related biosynthetic genes/pathway modules
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of floral scent biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive scent regulator; transcript reduced upon PhDEF suppression and promoter activated by PhDEF
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes floral scent production; positioned downstream of PhDEF
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary positive scent regulator activated by PhDEF
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR after PhDEF suppression; promoter activation assay using EOBIIp:DsRed with 35S:PhDEF
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EOBII transcript was significantly reduced in PhDEF-suppressed petals. 2. PhDEF activated the EOBII promoter in petals and leaves. 3. EOBII is a downstream regulatory node by which PhDEF activates phenylpropanoid scent production.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: EOBII promoter in reporter assay; 35S:PhDEF effector
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper frames EOBII as one of the major scent regulators activated by PhDEF.
